OPEN BDSet: a First-ever BDS Satellite-
based PPP augmentation Dataset
DATA DeSCRIPToR
Since 2023
Chenhao Ouyang 1,2, Junbo Shi1 ✉, Cheng Hou1, Wenjie Peng1, Jiming Guo1 & Yibin Yao1
the BDS precise point positioning (PPP) service broadcasts state space representation (SSR) corrections
via PPP-B2b augmentation signals. To maximize research potentials of the service, this study develops
a first-ever BDS PPP-B2b augmentation dataset since January 2023, named BDSet. This dataset
originates from PPP-B2b augmentation signals collected in Wuhan, China, and covers the pr

www.nature.com/scientificdata/ www.nature.com/scientificdata
corrections, which are then combined with the broadcast ephemeris to recover precise orbits, clock offsets and
DCBs15. The detailed recovery procedure is provided in the official BDS PPP-B2b ICD on pages 33 and 344.
Based on the recovered PPP-B2b precise products, numerous studies have been conducted, focusing on
product accuracy evaluation, positioning algorithm development, as well as zenith tropospheric delay (ZTD)
and precipitable water vapor (PWV) estimation. Regarding the product accuracy evaluation, existing stud-
ies have use

www.nature.com/scientificdata/ www.nature.com/scientificdata
Fig. 1 Configuration for the PPP-B2b augmentation data acquisition.
developed platform for PPP-B2b augmentation data acquisition is first introduced. Next, the dataset design is
explained, detailing the data processing workflow and data classification scheme. Then, a three-year dataset from
Jan. 2023 to Dec. 2025 is developed with implementation details including naming conventions, storage paths,
and formats of various files. Finally, the developed dataset is validated from several aspects, such as data size,
message amount, spatiot

Field Length Content
PPP-B2b 7 Denotes data from PPP-B2b augmentation signals
‘YYYY’: year
YYYYDOY (Date) 7
‘DOY’: day of year
‘MIX’: mixed messages from receiving chip
‘B2B’: PPP-B2b augmentation messages
‘SSR’: PPP-B2b SSR corrections
CCC (Content) 3
‘ORB’: PPP-B2b orbit
‘CLK’: PPP-B2b clock offset
‘DCB’: PPP-B2b DCB
‘RAW’: binary format defined by receiving chip
‘B2B’: binary format defined by PPP-B2b signal ICD32
‘SSR’: ASCII PPP-B2b SSR correction format defined by this study
FFF (Format) 3 ‘SP3’: SP3-d format defined by IGS34

GB, 16.64 GB, and 6.24 GB, respectively, totaling 43.61 GB. Taking files on January 1, 2023 as examples, the file

1-7, 2023.
Satellite mask Orbit correction DCB correction Clock correction
(Type 1) (Type 2) (Type 3) (Type 4)
(1) For Type 1697, the number of messages from January to May ranges between 123,135 and 172,800, and
then increases since June, ranging between 224,719 and 259,117. The increase in June is due to the launch
of the 4th BDS-3 GEO satellite C62 on May 17, 2023, which started broadcasting PPP-B2b augmentation
messages s

www.nature.com/scientificdata/ www.nature.com/scientificdata
evaluation of Level 1. Decoding of PPP-B2b augmentation messages in the sub-folder Level_1 shows that a
total of 78,246,531 PPP-B2b augmentation messages were collected in 2023, of which 78,238,323 passed the CRC
check. The message amounts from C59, C60, C61, and C62 are 30,098,337, 30,088,786, 95,477, and 17,955,723,
respectively. It should be noted that messages from C61 were only collected on January 19 and March 3, and no
message was collected from C61 during other periods. Therefore, subsequent discussions in this study exclude

www.nature.com/scientificdata/ www.nature.com/scientificdata
(2) For C62, the median latency of DCB corrections is 56.12 s, which is obviously higher than that of C59 and
C60 (17.12 s). This latency difference is attributed to the fact that C62 was in on-orbit testing phase, and
was not providing the same FOC PPP service as C59/C60 in 2023. Although the maximum clock offset
correction latencies occasionally exceed 20 s, the median value is 7.12 s, consistent with that of C59 and
C60. Median latencies for satellite mask and orbit corrections are 5.12 s and 24.12 s, which are also consist-
ent w
